title: Conservative safe SUPPORT recovery after Stage36 safety blockers
files_not_modified: Stage36 safety blocker behavior (Stage37 only reads Stage36's post-blocker output and cannot override a fired blocker)
Stage33-F structured owner behavior (only a new call site was added after Stage36's call site)

remaining_weakness_addressed.description: 'No X except Y ...' evidence entails Y SUPPORT (included-subset double-negative), left as REFUTE/NOT_ENTITLED after Stage36.
remaining_weakness_addressed.example_evidence: No workers except the night-shift workers were notified.

remaining_weakness_addressed.description: 'All X and all Z were P' evidence entails SUPPORT for a subset of X or Z, left as REFUTE/NOT_ENTITLED after Stage36.
remaining_weakness_addressed.example_evidence: All workers and all visitors were registered.

remaining_weakness_addressed.description: 'All N X were P' evidence entails SUPPORT for a subset among X, left as REFUTE/NOT_ENTITLED after Stage36.
remaining_weakness_addressed.example_claim: The night-shift workers among the workers were notified.

train_controlled_v6b_minimal.py.purpose: master switch to compute Stage37 safe SUPPORT recovery diagnostics; identical to Stage36-A when off
include stage37_* diagnostic fields in prediction exports
allow a fired safe-recovery rule to replace the exported shadow label; never touches final logits/predictions
when false, recover only from NOT_ENTITLED to SUPPORT; when true, also allow REFUTE to SUPPORT if the rule is exact and safety checks pass

safe_recovery_rule_summary.gating: Only fires when Stage37 is enabled, the post-Stage36 shadow label is NOT_ENTITLED or REFUTE (never already SUPPORT), no Stage36 support blocker fired on the row, and none of the four hazard checks trip. REFUTE recovery additionally requires --stage37-allow-recover-from-refute. Stage37 can never override a fired Stage36 blocker.
safe_recovery_rule_summary.no_except_included_subset_recovery: Detects 'no <whole> except <included> ... <predicate>' in evidence. Requires the claim's subject word-set to be an EXACT match (not merely overlapping) with the included-subset word-set, plus >=0.5 predicate word overlap. Exact match is required so that a distinguishing modifier swap (night-shift vs day-shift) -- which shares tokens like 'shift'/'workers' -- is never conflated with the actually-included subset.
safe_recovery_rule_summary.coordination_universal_subset_recovery: Detects 'all <first> and all <second> <predicate>' in evidence (literal 'and all', so 'and some' never matches). Recovers if the claim subject contains the head noun of <first> or <second> and predicate overlap >= 0.5.
safe_recovery_rule_summary.numeric_universal_subset_recovery: Detects 'all <number> <whole> <predicate>' in evidence (requires a leading 'all', so a bare '3 X were P' without 'all' never matches -- this specifically blocks reverse-numeric-overclaim). Recovers if the claim uses 'among the <whole>' phrasing or otherwise names a subset whose head is <whole>, plus predicate overlap >= 0.5.

integration_point.location: scripts/train_controlled_v6b_minimal.py: prediction_records_v6b(), inside the per-record export loop, immediately after the Stage36 support-safety-blocker block and before exported.append(item)
execution_order: 1. Stage33-F computes the proposed structured shadow label (composer_shadow.shadow_label).
2. Stage36 safety blockers may block unsafe SUPPORT (compute_stage36_support_safety_blocker).
3. Stage37 safe SUPPORT recovery may recover SUPPORT only if no Stage36 blocker fired on that row and no scope/exception/not-all hazard is present (compute_stage37_safe_support_recovery).
integration_point.inputs_used: record claim/evidence text, the post-Stage36 shadow label (Stage36's final_shadow_label when Stage36 ran, else the Stage33 composer_shadow label), the Stage36 result dict (for the fired-blocker gate), and the CLI args namespace
integration_point.output_written_to: item dict fields only (stage32_shadow_label / stage33_conditional_shadow_label overwritten only in shadow_mode when a Stage37 rule fired; stage37_* diagnostic fields added only when export is enabled). stage36_final_shadow_label and other pre-existing Stage33/Stage36 fields are never overwritten by Stage37.

evaluator_changes.shadow_label_resolution: resolve_shadow_label() now prefers stage37_final_shadow_label, then stage36_final_shadow_label, then stage32_shadow_label, then stage33_conditional_shadow_label. All normal Stage35/36 counters (adv_*, group/family/perturbation metrics) automatically reflect the post-Stage37 label because they all route through resolve_shadow_label(). resolve_shadow_label_pre_stage37() (new) prefers stage37_original_shadow_label, else stage36_final_shadow_label, for the pre-Stage37 (post-Stage36) view used in before/after diagnostics.
evaluator_changes.backward_compatibility: When no row carries stage37_safe_recovery_fired, stage37_fields_present is false, no stage37_* keys are added to the report, and the Stage35/Stage36 decision labels are computed exactly as before (verified against a synthetic predictions file carrying only stage36_* fields).

verification_performed.type: local unit-level smoke tests only (no Kaggle run, no training run)
checks: module import smoke test of scripts/train_controlled_v6b_minimal.py

_stage37_no_except_included_subset_recovery / _stage37_coordination_universal_subset_recovery / _stage37_numeric_universal_subset_recovery tested against the positive examples from the design spec (all fired as expected) and the explicit negative examples (day-shift vs night-shift non-included subset, unrelated coordination target, reverse-numeric-overclaim; all correctly did not fire)
has_excluded_subset_hazard / has_not_all_existential_hazard / has_location_scope_mismatch verified to trip on the corresponding hazard patterns

compute_stage37_safe_support_recovery() verified: no-op when Stage37 disabled (default), fires on NOT_ENTITLED->SUPPORT when enabled, never fires when a Stage36 blocker already fired on the row, gated off for REFUTE->SUPPORT unless --stage37-allow-recover-from-refute is set (then fires), no-op when already SUPPORT, blocked by exception hazard on an all-except-excluded-subset claim

ran evaluate_stage35_adversarial_coverage.py end-to-end on a synthetic predictions file with stage37_* fields present: resolve_shadow_label priority, before/after counters, and STAGE37A_SAFE_SUPPORT_RECOVERY_EFFECTIVE decision matched expectations

ran evaluate_stage35_adversarial_coverage.py end-to-end on a synthetic predictions file simulating unsafe Stage37 recovery (overclaim reappearing): STAGE37A_RECOVERY_TOO_UNSAFE decision correctly triggered

ran evaluate_stage35_adversarial_coverage.py end-to-end on a synthetic legacy predictions file with only stage36_* fields (no stage37_* fields): stage37_fields_present is false, no stage37_* keys appear, and the existing stage36_decision path is unaffected

remaining_risks: passive/active-voice SUPPORT recovery is not addressed by any Stage37-A rule and remains unresolved

coordination parsing (--stage37-recover-coordination-universal-subset) is heuristic head-noun overlap, not real parsing, and is deliberately loose per the design spec (e.g. 'night-shift workers' matches 'workers'); this could overclaim on contrived multi-word whole phrases sharing a head noun with a different qualifier, and should be monitored via stage37_blocked_by_*_count and stage37_post_overclaim_to_support on every run

numeric subset recovery (--stage37-recover-numeric-universal-subset) is synthetic in that it depends on a specific 'among the X' claim phrasing or exact head-noun subset phrasing; other real-world phrasings expressing the same fact will not be recovered
Stage37 remains shadow-only and diagnostic-only: it must not be used for training, calibration, threshold selection, loss, checkpoint selection, or Kaggle selection

all Stage35-A/Stage36-A baseline numbers in this report are carried over from the task's previously reported Kaggle run; actual post-Stage37-A numbers require a fresh Kaggle validation run per task instructions
